title: Perl perf issue in =~ (binding operator) on verilated.cpp
body:
An off-topic (not Verilator issue) - on regression runs in test_regress.

On a CentOS I see t_dist_cppstyle.pl hangs for hours together. With some prints I located the issue to be around "binding operator" in Perl - for some big (not really big) file such as verilated.cpp

```perl
  printf ( "$filename $message $pattern %0d \n", length($buffer));
    while ($buffer =~ s/.*?^($pattern)//sm) {
      print "AsFigo2: $filename $message \n";
```
I got below:
```log
 ../include/verilated.cpp 'virtual' keyword is redundant on 'override' method (?^:[^/]*virtual[^{};]+override) 130126 
```
I don't see the 2nd printf inside that while-loop executing at all (even after 4-5 hours). Sure, this laptop is not the top-of-the-line config/fully loaded, but it does run quite a few EDA tools reasonably well. 

For experimentation sake, I emptied verilated.cpp and the issue shows up on next file. 

Any clues on how we can fix it cleanly please?
